message:
feat: extract agent-detect crate, overhaul TUI dashboard, and add post-cutover cleanup tooling

A major milestone combining three streams of work across the
mcp-agent-mail-rust workspace (+2,894 lines, -1,517 lines):

Agent detection extraction:
- New crate: mcp-agent-mail-agent-detect/ — extracts the agent
  detection logic from mcp-agent-mail-core into a standalone crate
  with its own Cargo.toml and src/lib.rs, enabling downstream
  consumers to depend on detection without pulling in the full core
- Slim mcp-agent-mail-core/src/agent_detect.rs by ~309 lines,
  replacing the implementation with a thin re-export facade
- Update mcp-agent-mail-core/Cargo.toml to depend on the new crate
- Adjust config_env_otel.rs and schema_migration.rs tests for the
  new module boundaries

TUI dashboard overhaul (the bulk of the change):
- dashboard.rs: +1,286 lines — add live quick-query input (/ to
  focus), reservation gauges with expiry-soon highlighting, agent
  status tiles with active/idle/stale classification, project
  summary cards, contact summary rendering, recent-message preview
  panel, top-match sampling, and quick-filter control labels
- lib.rs: +716 lines of server-side supporting infrastructure
  (AgentSummary, ContactSummary, ProjectSummary, ReservationSnapshot
  types fed from the poller to the TUI)
- tui_poller.rs: +167 lines expanding the polling loop to collect
  agent activity, reservation snapshots, and project metadata for
  the new dashboard panels
- tui_chrome.rs: refine chrome layout for the expanded dashboard
- tui_bridge.rs: add new shared-state fields for dashboard data
- tui_focus.rs, tui_hit_regions.rs: wire focus and mouse targets
  for new interactive dashboard elements
- tui_events.rs: add new event types and shared-state structs
- tui_app.rs: integrate dashboard lifecycle hooks
- tui_theme.rs: add theme tokens for reservation gauges and agent
  status badges
- reservations.rs: +256 lines expanding the reservations screen
  with filterable views
- search.rs: +128 lines improving search screen interaction
- Other screen modules: minor alignment fixes

Accessibility & tests:
- scripts/e2e_tui_a11y.sh: expand accessibility E2E test suite
- scripts/e2e/post_cutover_cleanup_agent_mail.sh: new 10KB script
  automating post-cutover decommission of the old agent-detect path
- docs/post_cutover_cleanup/: decommission manifest, validation
  report, and replay command for audit trail

Infrastructure:
- Cargo.lock: dependency tree refresh (-916 lines net from crate
  consolidation)
- Cargo.toml: add workspace member for new agent-detect crate
- README.md: minor version/feature updates
- CLI: fix lib.rs import path for extracted crate
- Search: add safety comments in fs_bridge, fusion, two_tier
- Test fixtures: update golden snapshots and CLI help texts

Beads: sync issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A clear, predictable token/auth startup model for `am serve`.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:25:04.832126845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:26:17.319039177Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:26:17.319018108Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":218,"issue_id":"br-3n3v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T5.2: Implement Robust .env File Token Loader

## What to build
Parse .env files to extract HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN. Replaces the grep/sed/cut pipeline
in scripts/am (lines 58-77, 171-183).

## Current bash behavior
```bash
token=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN=" "$file" | tail -n1 | cut -d= -f2-)
token=$(echo "$token" | sed -e 's/^[[:space:]]*//' -e 's/[[:space:]]*$//')
token=$(echo "$token" | sed -e 's/^"//' -e 's/"$//' -e "s/^'//" -e "s/'$//")
```

## Rust implementation
```rust
struct EnvFile {
    entries: BTreeMap<String, String>,
}

impl EnvFile {
    fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;
        let mut entries = BTreeMap::new();
        for line in content.lines() {
            let trimmed = line.trim();
            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {
                continue;  // skip empty lines and comments
            }
            if let Some((key, value)) = trimmed.split_once('=') {
                let key = key.trim().to_string();
                let mut value = value.trim().to_string();
                // Strip surrounding quotes
                if (value.starts_with('"') && value.ends_with('"'))
                    || (value.starts_with('\'') && value.ends_with('\''))
                {
                    value = value[1..value.len()-1].to_string();
                }
                entries.insert(key, value);
            }
        }
        Ok(Self { entries })
    }

    fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<&str> {
        self.entries.get(key).map(|s| s.as_str())
    }
}

/// Load token using the same search path as scripts/am
fn load_bearer_token(env_file_override: Option<&Path>) -> Option<String> {
    // 1. Check env var first
    if let Ok(token) = std::env::var("HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN") {
        if !token.is_empty() { return Some(token); }
    }
    // 2. Try env file
    let path = env_file_override.map(PathBuf::from).unwrap_or_else(|| {
        let preferred = dirs::home_dir().unwrap().join(".mcp_agent_mail/.env");
        let legacy = dirs::home_dir().unwrap().join("mcp_agent_mail/.env");
        if preferred.exists() { preferred } else { legacy }
    });
    let env = EnvFile::load(&path).ok()?;
    env.get("HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN").map(String::from)
}
```

## Implementation notes
- Handle edge cases: no trailing newline, Windows line endings (\r\n), UTF-8 BOM
- Support multi-line values? Probably not needed for .env files, keep it simple
- The two search paths (preferred: ~/.mcp_agent_mail/.env, fallback: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env)
  must match scripts/am's default_env_file() function
- Note: dirs::home_dir() or std::env::var("HOME") — prefer HOME env var to avoid
  adding a dirs dependency

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/env_loader.rs (new module) or startup_checks.rs

","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:51:01Z"},{"id":272,"issue_id":"br-3n3v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: Track 5 Real Gaps

### T5.2 — .env File Search is a REAL GAP
The Rust config.rs loads .env from the CURRENT WORKING DIRECTORY only.
The bash scripts/am searches these specific paths:
  1. ~/.mcp_agent_mail/.env (preferred)
  2. ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env (legacy, no dot prefix)

This is a REAL gap — when agents run from their project directory (e.g., /data/projects/foo),
the CWD .env won't have the Agent Mail token. The bash wrapper handles this by
explicitly searching the home-directory-based paths.

T5.2 should add the home-directory .env search to Config::from_env() or
to the serve command startup, using the same precedence as bash:
  1. Environment variable (already works)
  2. ~/.mcp_agent_mail/.env
  3. ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env
  4. CWD .env (already works)

### T5.1 — Port Detection Limitations
The bash uses lsof to get the PID of the listener. TcpListener::bind() only tells
you if the port is available, NOT what process holds it.

For full parity, Rust needs to either:
a) Shell out to `lsof -tiTCP:{port} -sTCP:LISTEN` (platform-specific but matches bash)
b) Read /proc/net/tcp on Linux (more robust, no external dep)
c) Just try to bind and report the error (simpler but loses PID info)

Option (a) is recommended for parity. The PID is needed to check if the
existing process is mcp_agent_mail / mcp-agent-mail (vs a foreign process).

### T5.4 — --env-file flag
The bash supports --env-file <path> to override the default .env search.
Check if the Rust main.rs already has this flag. If not, add it.

 {"id":"br-3nbef","title":"[track] T8: Focus Graph & Spatial Navigation Overhaul","description":"Implement frankentui's full focus management system for professional spatial navigation
between panels, replacing ad-hoc focus tracking with a proper focus graph.

CURRENT STATE:
- Focus tracking is manual per-screen (each screen manages its own focus_pane index)
- Tab cycles through panes in order
- No spatial navigation (arrow keys don't move focus between panels)
- Focus state resets on screen switch

FRANKENTUI FOCUS SYSTEM:
- Focus graph: directed graph of focusable widgets
- Spatial navigation: arrow keys move focus to nearest widget in direction
- Focus memory: remembers last focused widget per screen
- Focus indicators: visual border/highlight changes on focus
- Focus traps: modal/overlay captures focus until dismissed
- Tab order: explicit or auto-computed from layout

DESIGN:
1. Define focus graph for each screen's panel layout
2. Arrow keys (when not consumed by widget) move focus spatially
3. Tab cycles through focus graph in document order
4. Focus state persisted in screen model
5. Focused panel gets palette.panel_border_focused border
6. Unfocused panels get palette.panel_border_dim border
7. Modal overlays trap focus (already partially done)

This is foundational — forms (T9) and drag-and-drop (T13) depend on proper focus management.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Focus graph defined for all 14 screens
- [ ] Arrow keys navigate between panels spatially
- [ ] Tab cycles through panels in document order
- [ ] Focus remembered per screen across screen switches
- [ ] Focused panel border uses panel_border_focused
- [ ] Modals trap focus until dismissed
- [ ] Focus graph updated when layout changes (dock/undock)

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:33:08.049806854Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["focus","frankentui","navigation","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3nbef","depends_on_id":"br-1xt0m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:57.930606118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":587,"issue_id":"br-3nbef","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FOCUS SYSTEM DESIGN (2026-02-13, RubyPrairie):

WHY THIS IS FOUNDATIONAL:

The focus system is the unsung hero of professional TUI applications. Currently, each screen
manages focus manually via a focus_pane index (typically an enum like FocusPane::Left, FocusPane::Right).
This works but creates problems:
1. Focus doesn't persist across screen switches (resets to default)
2. No spatial navigation (can't press Right arrow to move from left pane to right pane)
3. No focus traps for modals (partially implemented but ad-hoc)
4. Adding a new panel requires updating the enum and all match arms

Frankentui's focus graph solves all of these. Key concepts:
- FocusNode: A focusable widget identified by a stable ID
- FocusGraph: Directed graph connecting nodes with spatial relationships
- FocusMemory: Per-screen storage of last focused node
- FocusTrap: Captures focus within a modal until dismissed

IMPLEMENTATION STRATEGY:

PHASE 1 (T8.1): Define focus graphs statically for each screen. Each screen's model gets
a FocusGraph field constructed from the panel layout. Panel Rects computed during view()
feed into the graph's spatial relationships.

PHASE 2 (T8.2): Wire keyboard handling. Ctrl+Arrow navigates spatially (using Ctrl to avoid
conflicting with widget-level arrow keys). Tab cycles in document order. Focus memory saves
and restores per screen.

WHY Ctrl+Arrow INSTEAD OF BARE Arrow:
Many widgets consume arrow keys (lists scroll, text areas move cursor). Using Ctrl+Arrow
for focus navigation avoids all conflicts. This is the VS Code pattern (Ctrl+Arrow moves
between editor groups, bare Arrow moves within editor). It is already muscle memory for many
developers.

DEPENDENCY NOTE:
Track 9 (Forms) and Track 13 (DnD) both depend on this because forms need Tab order between
fields and DnD needs focus-aware drop zones.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:10:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3np","title":"CLI: share export/update parity (interactive + defaults)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share export` and `share update` commands with legacy behavior (including `--interactive`).

## Scope
- Flags: `--output/-o`, `--interactive/-i`, `--project/-p` (repeat), thresholds, scrub preset, chunk settings, `--dry-run`, `--zip`, `--signing-key`, `--signing-public-out`, `--age-recipient` (repeat).
- Defaults: inline=64KiB, detach=25MiB, chunk threshold=20MiB, chunk size=4MiB; scrub preset `standard`.
- Validation: invalid preset exits 1; enforce chunk size >=1024; auto‑adjust detach threshold if <= inline.
- `share export`:
  - `--dry-run` creates temp output + prints summary only.
  - `--interactive` wizard prompts and overrides flags.
- `share update`:
  - loads config from existing `manifest.json` and stored export config.
  - default `--zip` is **false** (contrast export default true).

## Tests

WHY A TREE WIDGET FOR THREADS:

Current thread display is a flat list of messages. When agent conversations are deep
(10+ messages with nested replies), it's extremely difficult to understand the reply
structure and find specific sub-conversations.

The frankentui Tree widget with 5 guide styles (Plain, Rounded, Bold, Double, ASCII) provides
exactly the hierarchical navigation needed. Key advantages:
1. Visual reply depth at a glance (indentation + guides)
2. Collapse old branches to focus on recent activity
3. Expand specific sub-conversations without losing context
4. Natural keyboard navigation (Left=collapse, Right=expand)

TREE CONSTRUCTION FROM FLAT MESSAGES:

Messages in Agent Mail have:
- message_id: unique ID
- reply_to_id: optional, references parent message
- thread_id: groups messages into threads

Algorithm:
1. Collect all messages in thread
2. Build parent->children map using reply_to_id
3. Messages with no reply_to_id are root nodes
4. Sort children by created_at ascending
5. Build recursive TreeItem structure

Edge cases:
- reply_to_id references non-existent message: treat as root
- Circular references (shouldn't happen but): detect and break cycle
- Very deep nesting (> 20 levels): flatten beyond depth 20

GUIDE STYLE: Use Rounded by default (matches BorderType::Rounded used throughout the TUI).
Allow override via env var AM_TUI_TREE_STYLE=rounded|plain|bold|double|ascii.

INTEGRATION WITH EXISTING THREADS SCREEN:
The Threads screen currently has a list pane and a preview pane. Replace the list pane
with the Tree widget. Selection in the tree updates the preview pane. This is a drop-in
replacement that preserves the existing UX while adding hierarchical navigation.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:10:23Z"}]}

Native 'am flake-triage' fully replaces scripts/flake_triage.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-h1yu","depends_on_id":"br-137e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:37:16.920649034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"br-h1yu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 2: am flake-triage — Native Flake Analysis

## What it replaces
scripts/flake_triage.sh (194 lines of bash)

## Current behavior
Three modes of operation:
1. **reproduce**: Re-run a specific test from a failure artifact JSON file
2. **scan**: Walk directory trees to find failure artifact files, summarize them
3. **detect**: Multi-seed flake detection — run a test N times with different seeds,
   report if it passes/fails intermittently (true flake vs hard failure)

All JSON parsing uses inline Python one-liners like:
  python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$file')); print(d.get('test_name',''))"

## External dependencies eliminated
- **python3**: Every JSON operation uses an inline Python script. Rust's serde_json
  replaces this entirely.

## Key improvements
1. **Existing Rust module**: crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/flake_triage.rs already has
   domain logic for flake analysis. The bash script is a less-capable duplicate. This
   track wires the existing module into the CLI.
2. **Structured output**: Instead of printing text, produce JSON with test names,
   failure rates, seed values, and reproduction commands.
3. **Parallel multi-seed**: The detect mode can run N seeds in parallel using
   std::process::Command with thread pooling, much faster than bash's sequential loop.
4. **Cross-platform**: No bash-specific path handling or process management.

## CLI interface
```
am flake-triage scan [--dir <path>] [--json]
am flake-triage reproduce <artifact.json> [--json]
am flake-triage detect <test_name> [--seeds N] [--parallel] [--json]
```

## Implementation location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/flake_triage.rs (new module, wraps core::flake_triage)
Wire into Cli enum in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

## Files to read for context
- scripts/flake_triage.sh (the script being replaced)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/flake_triage.rs (existing Rust module to wrap)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (CLI structure to extend)

 {"id":"br-h8xsy","title":"G.3: Wire into tool metrics screen + TUI display","description":"**Background**

The BOCPD detector and conformal predictor from G.1 and G.2 must be integrated into the tool metrics system and displayed in the TUI.

**Scope / Adoption wedge**

1. Add `BocpdDetector` and `ConformalPredictor` instances per tool in the metrics system.
2. On each tool call completion, feed the latency to both the BOCPD detector and conformal predictor.
3. When a change point is detected, emit an `AnomalyCard` to the TUI's anomaly feed.
4. Display conformal prediction intervals in the `PercentileRibbon` widget as shaded bands.
5. Add a "Change Points" column to the tool metrics table showing the count and last change-point timestamp.
6. Record all change-point detections and conformal interval updates to the evidence ledger.

**Risks / Safe Mode**

- Risk: Per-tool BOCPD state consumes memory. Mitigation: max_run_length=500, ~4KB per tool. With 34 tools, total < 140KB.
- Fallback trigger: Feature flag `metrics-bocpd` (default: enabled).

**Tests (5 required)**

1. `metrics_bocpd_integration` -- simulate tool calls with latency shift, verify change point detected
2. `metrics_conformal_intervals_displayed` -- verify PercentileRibbon renders with conformal bands
3. `metrics_anomaly_card_emitted` -- change point triggers AnomalyCard creation
4. `metrics_evidence_recorded` -- change points and intervals logged to evidence ledger
5. `metrics_per_tool_isolation` -- change point in tool A does not affect tool B's state","design":"**Logging requirements:**
- `info!("metrics_bocpd: tool={} bocpd+conformal state created", tool_name)` on lazy init
- `debug!("metrics_bocpd: tool={} change_point detected, anomaly_card emitted", tool_name)` on detection
- `debug!("metrics_bocpd: tool={} conformal interval [{:.1}, {:.1}] coverage={:.0}%", tool, lo, hi, coverage*100)` on interval update
- `trace!("metrics_bocpd: tool={} latency_ms={:.1} observations={}", tool_name, lat, obs_count)` per observation
- `warn!("metrics_bocpd: tool={} insufficient data ({} < {}), skipping conformal", tool, obs, min_window)` on fallback","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Per-tool BOCPD and conformal instances are wired into live tool-latency pipeline
- Unit tests validate update ordering, state lifecycle, and per-tool isolation
- Integration tests verify anomaly cards, interval overlays, and metrics table counters remain coherent
- E2E PTY scenario injects tool anomalies and validates operator-visible alerts + intervals
- Evidence ledger integration captures anomaly decisions and follow-up outcomes
- Feature flag metrics-bocpd is tested in enabled and disabled modes
- Diagnostics include tool identifier, anomaly score, interval bounds, and render latency impact","notes":"## Plan-Space Review Findings (2026-02-13)

**CRITICAL: No file paths or function signatures.** This is a wiring bead that lacks implementation specifics. Add:

- **Per-tool state lives in:** `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/tool_metrics.rs` — add `HashMap<String, (BocpdDetector, ConformalPredictor)>` to the screen model.
- **Latency feeding happens in:** `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tool_dispatch.rs` (or wherever tool call timing is measured) — after each tool completes, call `detector.update(latency_ms)` and `predictor.update(latency_ms)`.
- **AnomalyCard emission:** Use the existing `AnomalyCard` widget at `tui_widgets.rs` lines 1271+. Create via `AnomalyCard::new(tool_name, "Change point detected", severity, details)`.
- **PercentileRibbon integration:** `tui_widgets.rs` lines 808+. Add `conformal_lower` and `conformal_upper` fields to the ribbon's input data struct.
- **Change points column:** Add to the table in `tool_metrics.rs` screen — new column "CPs" showing `detector.change_points().len()`.

**Per-tool lifecycle:** Create `BocpdDetector` on first tool call (lazy init). Never destroy — tools do not deregister. Memory is bounded by `max_run_length=500` per tool.

**Rate limiting for anomaly cards:** Add a cooldown of 60 seconds per tool between AnomalyCard emissions. Use `HashMap<String, Instant>` for last-emission timestamps. This prevents flooding the TUI with cards during a sustained regime shift.

**BOCPD-triggered conformal window reset:** When BOCPD detects a change point, call `predictor.reset_window()` to flush stale calibration scores from the previous regime. This is the key cross-component interaction.

**Additional test:**
6. `metrics_anomaly_rate_limited` — two change points within 30 seconds, only one AnomalyCard emitted

## Refinement Pass 2 Findings (2026-02-14)

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# post_cutover_cleanup_agent_mail.sh - deterministic post-cutover cleanup verifier
+#
+# Required by bd-3un.37.2 acceptance criteria.
+# Verifies retained frankensearch search path behavior and emits deterministic
+# artifacts for replay/debug.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
+LOG_ROOT="${ROOT_DIR}/test_logs/post_cutover_cleanup"
+
+MODE="smoke"
+EXECUTION_MODE="live"
+
+usage() {
+  cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: scripts/e2e/post_cutover_cleanup_agent_mail.sh [--mode smoke|all] [--execution live|dry] [--dry-run]
+
+Options:
+  --mode <smoke|all>      smoke: compile/lint/unit/db checks, all: smoke + Search V3 stdio e2e.
+  --execution <live|dry>  live executes commands, dry records deterministic skipped-stage artifacts.
+  --dry-run               Alias for --execution dry.
+  -h, --help              Show this help text.
+
+Environment:
+  POST_CUTOVER_FORCE_LOCAL_CIRCUIT=1  Prefixes cargo stages with RCH_MOCK_CIRCUIT_OPEN=1.
+USAGE
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --mode)
+      MODE="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --execution)
+      EXECUTION_MODE="$2"
+      shift 2
+      ;;
+    --dry-run)
+      EXECUTION_MODE="dry"
+      shift
+      ;;
+    -h|--help)
+      usage
+      exit 0
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
+      usage >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+if [[ "$MODE" != "smoke" && "$MODE" != "all" ]]; then
+  echo "Invalid --mode: $MODE (expected smoke|all)" >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+if [[ "$EXECUTION_MODE" != "live" && "$EXECUTION_MODE" != "dry" ]]; then
+  echo "Invalid --execution: $EXECUTION_MODE (expected live|dry)" >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+json_escape() {
+  python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.dumps(sys.argv[1])[1:-1])' "$1"
+}
+
+now_iso() {
+  date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'
+}
+
+RUN_ID="post-cutover-cleanup-agent-mail-$(date -u '+%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ')-$(date +%s%N)-$$"
+RUN_DIR="${LOG_ROOT}/${RUN_ID}"
+RUN_SUFFIX="${RUN_ID##*-}"
+CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${POST_CUTOVER_CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-/data/tmp/target-post-cutover-cleanup-${RUN_SUFFIX}}"
+EVENTS_JSONL="${RUN_DIR}/events.jsonl"
+TRANSCRIPT_TXT="${RUN_DIR}/terminal_transcript.txt"
+SUMMARY_JSON="${RUN_DIR}/summary.json"
+SUMMARY_MD="${RUN_DIR}/summary.md"
+MANIFEST_JSON="${RUN_DIR}/manifest.json"
+REPLAY_TXT="${RUN_DIR}/decommission_replay_command.txt"
+
+mkdir -p "${RUN_DIR}"
+: > "${EVENTS_JSONL}"
+: > "${TRANSCRIPT_TXT}"
+
+cat > "${REPLAY_TXT}" <<EOF_REPLAY
+cd ${ROOT_DIR} && POST_CUTOVER_CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${CARGO_TARGET_DIR} POST_CUTOVER_FORCE_LOCAL_CIRCUIT=${POST_CUTOVER_FORCE_LOCAL_CIRCUIT:-0} scripts/e2e/post_cutover_cleanup_agent_mail.sh --mode ${MODE} --execution ${EXECUTION_MODE}
+EOF_REPLAY
+
+STAGE_STARTED_COUNT=0
+STAGE_COMPLETED_COUNT=0
+ACTIVE_STAGE=""
+RUN_STATUS="ok"
+RUN_REASON_CODE="post_cutover_cleanup.session.ok"
+RUN_MESSAGE="session initialized"
+LAST_FAILURE_STAGE=""
+LAST_FAILURE_EXIT_CODE=""
+LAST_FAILURE_REASON_CODE=""
+FINALIZED=0
+
+if [[ "${POST_CUTOVER_FORCE_LOCAL_CIRCUIT:-0}" == "1" ]]; then
+  RCH_PREFIX="RCH_MOCK_CIRCUIT_OPEN=1 rch exec --"
+else
+  RCH_PREFIX="rch exec --"
+fi
+
+emit_event() {
+  local stage="$1"
+  local status="$2"
+  local reason_code="$3"
+  local detail="$4"
+  printf '{"run_id":"%s","ts":"%s","mode":"%s","execution_mode":"%s","stage":"%s","status":"%s","reason_code":"%s","detail":"%s"}\n' \
+    "$(json_escape "${RUN_ID}")" \
+    "$(now_iso)" \
+    "$(json_escape "${MODE}")" \
+    "$(json_escape "${EXECUTION_MODE}")" \
+    "$(json_escape "${stage}")" \
+    "$(json_escape "${status}")" \
+    "$(json_escape "${reason_code}")" \
+    "$(json_escape "${detail}")" \
+    >> "${EVENTS_JSONL}"
+}
+
+record_command_header() {
+  local stage="$1"
+  local cmd="$2"
+  {
+    printf '\n[%s] stage=%s command=%s\n' "$(now_iso)" "${stage}" "${cmd}"
+  } >> "${TRANSCRIPT_TXT}"
+}
+
+mark_stage_started() {
+  local stage="$1"
+  ACTIVE_STAGE="$stage"
+  STAGE_STARTED_COUNT=$((STAGE_STARTED_COUNT + 1))
+}
+
+mark_stage_completed() {
+  ACTIVE_STAGE=""
+  STAGE_COMPLETED_COUNT=$((STAGE_COMPLETED_COUNT + 1))
+}
+
+set_status() {
+  RUN_STATUS="$1"
+  RUN_REASON_CODE="$2"
+  RUN_MESSAGE="$3"
+}
+
+classify_stage_failure_reason() {
+  local exit_code="$1"
+  if [[ "$exit_code" -eq 124 || "$exit_code" -eq 137 || "$exit_code" -eq 143 ]]; then
+    printf '%s' "post_cutover_cleanup.stage.timeout"
+    return
+  fi
+  printf '%s' "post_cutover_cleanup.stage.failed"
+}
+
+run_stage() {
+  local stage="$1"
+  local cmd="$2"
+  mark_stage_started "$stage"
+  emit_event "$stage" "running" "post_cutover_cleanup.stage.started" "Stage started"
+
+  if [[ "$EXECUTION_MODE" == "dry" ]]; then
+    record_command_header "$stage" "$cmd"
+    printf '[dry-run] skipped execution\n' >> "${TRANSCRIPT_TXT}"
+    emit_event "$stage" "ok" "post_cutover_cleanup.stage.skipped_dry_run" "Dry-run mode"
+    mark_stage_completed
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  record_command_header "$stage" "$cmd"
+  set +e
+  bash -lc "cd \"${ROOT_DIR}\" && ${cmd}" >> "${TRANSCRIPT_TXT}" 2>&1
+  local exit_code=$?
+  set -e
+
+  if [[ "$exit_code" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    emit_event "$stage" "ok" "post_cutover_cleanup.stage.ok" "Stage completed"
+    mark_stage_completed
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  local reason
+  reason="$(classify_stage_failure_reason "$exit_code")"
+  LAST_FAILURE_STAGE="$stage"
+  LAST_FAILURE_EXIT_CODE="$exit_code"
+  LAST_FAILURE_REASON_CODE="$reason"
+  emit_event "$stage" "fail" "$reason" "Command failed (exit=${exit_code})"
+  mark_stage_completed
+  return "$exit_code"
+}
+
+write_summary() {
+  cat > "${SUMMARY_JSON}" <<EOF_SUMMARY
+{"schema":"post-cutover-cleanup-summary-v1","v":1,"run_id":"$(json_escape "${RUN_ID}")","status":"$(json_escape "${RUN_STATUS}")","reason_code":"$(json_escape "${RUN_REASON_CODE}")","message":"$(json_escape "${RUN_MESSAGE}")","mode":"$(json_escape "${MODE}")","execution_mode":"$(json_escape "${EXECUTION_MODE}")","cargo_target_dir":"$(json_escape "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}")","stage_started_count":${STAGE_STARTED_COUNT},"stage_completed_count":${STAGE_COMPLETED_COUNT},"active_stage":"$(json_escape "${ACTIVE_STAGE}")","last_failure_stage":"$(json_escape "${LAST_FAILURE_STAGE}")","last_failure_exit_code":"$(json_escape "${LAST_FAILURE_EXIT_CODE}")","events":"$(json_escape "${EVENTS_JSONL}")","transcript":"$(json_escape "${TRANSCRIPT_TXT}")","replay":"$(json_escape "${REPLAY_TXT}")","ts":"$(now_iso)"}
+EOF_SUMMARY
+
+  cat > "${SUMMARY_MD}" <<EOF_SUMMARY_MD
+# Post-Cutover Cleanup Validation Summary
+
+- run_id: \`${RUN_ID}\`
+- status: **${RUN_STATUS}**
+- reason_code: \`${RUN_REASON_CODE}\`
+- mode: \`${MODE}\`
+- execution_mode: \`${EXECUTION_MODE}\`
+- cargo_target_dir: \`${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}\`
+- stage_started_count: ${STAGE_STARTED_COUNT}
+- stage_completed_count: ${STAGE_COMPLETED_COUNT}
+- active_stage: \`${ACTIVE_STAGE:-<none>}\`
+- last_failure_stage: \`${LAST_FAILURE_STAGE:-<none>}\`
+- last_failure_exit_code: \`${LAST_FAILURE_EXIT_CODE:-<none>}\`
+
+## Artifacts
+
+- events: \`${EVENTS_JSONL}\`
+- transcript: \`${TRANSCRIPT_TXT}\`
+- replay: \`${REPLAY_TXT}\`
+- summary_json: \`${SUMMARY_JSON}\`
+- manifest: \`${MANIFEST_JSON}\`
+
+EOF_SUMMARY_MD
+
+  cat > "${MANIFEST_JSON}" <<EOF_MANIFEST
+{"schema":"post-cutover-cleanup-manifest-v1","v":1,"run_id":"$(json_escape "${RUN_ID}")","status":"$(json_escape "${RUN_STATUS}")","reason_code":"$(json_escape "${RUN_REASON_CODE}")","mode":"$(json_escape "${MODE}")","execution_mode":"$(json_escape "${EXECUTION_MODE}")","cargo_target_dir":"$(json_escape "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}")","stage_started_count":${STAGE_STARTED_COUNT},"stage_completed_count":${STAGE_COMPLETED_COUNT},"active_stage":"$(json_escape "${ACTIVE_STAGE}")","artifacts":{"events":"$(json_escape "${EVENTS_JSONL}")","transcript":"$(json_escape "${TRANSCRIPT_TXT}")","replay":"$(json_escape "${REPLAY_TXT}")","summary_json":"$(json_escape "${SUMMARY_JSON}")","summary_md":"$(json_escape "${SUMMARY_MD}")"},"ts":"$(now_iso)"}
+EOF_MANIFEST
+}
+
+finalize() {
+  local exit_code="$1"
+  if [[ "$FINALIZED" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    return
+  fi
+  FINALIZED=1
+
+  if [[ "$exit_code" -ne 0 && "$RUN_STATUS" == "ok" ]]; then
+    if [[ -n "$LAST_FAILURE_REASON_CODE" ]]; then
+      set_status "fail" "$LAST_FAILURE_REASON_CODE" "Stage failed: ${LAST_FAILURE_STAGE} (exit=${LAST_FAILURE_EXIT_CODE})"
+    else
+      set_status "fail" "post_cutover_cleanup.session.failed" "Session failed"
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  write_summary
+  emit_event "session.finalize" "$RUN_STATUS" "$RUN_REASON_CODE" "$RUN_MESSAGE"
+
+  if [[ "$RUN_STATUS" == "ok" ]]; then
+    printf 'Artifacts: %s\n' "$RUN_DIR"
+  else
+    printf 'Artifacts (failed run): %s\n' "$RUN_DIR" >&2
+  fi
+}
+
+on_signal() {
+  local signal_name="$1"
+  set_status "fail" "post_cutover_cleanup.session.interrupted" "Received signal ${signal_name}"
+  emit_event "session.interrupted" "fail" "post_cutover_cleanup.session.interrupted" "Interrupted by ${signal_name}"
+  exit 143
+}
+
+on_exit() {
+  local rc=$?
+  finalize "$rc"
+  exit "$rc"
+}
+
+trap 'on_signal INT' INT
+trap 'on_signal TERM' TERM
+trap 'on_signal HUP' HUP
+trap on_exit EXIT
+
+emit_event "session.init" "ok" "post_cutover_cleanup.session.init" "Session initialized"
+
+run_stage "compile.search_core_hybrid" "env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${CARGO_TARGET_DIR} ${RCH_PREFIX} cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --all-targets --features hybrid"
+run_stage "lint.search_core_hybrid" "env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${CARGO_TARGET_DIR} ${RCH_PREFIX} cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --all-targets --features hybrid -- -D warnings"
+run_stage "unit.search_core.fs_probe_doc_key_roundtrip" "env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${CARGO_TARGET_DIR} ${RCH_PREFIX} cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --features hybrid fs_probe_doc_key_roundtrip -- --nocapture"
+run_stage "unit.search_core.from_fs_phase" "env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${CARGO_TARGET_DIR} ${RCH_PREFIX} cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --features hybrid from_fs_phase_ -- --nocapture"
+run_stage "integration.db.select_best_two_tier_results" "env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${CARGO_TARGET_DIR} ${RCH_PREFIX} cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db --features hybrid select_best_two_tier_results -- --nocapture"
+
+if [[ "$MODE" == "all" ]]; then
+  run_stage "e2e.search_v3_stdio" "env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${CARGO_TARGET_DIR} ${RCH_PREFIX} bash tests/e2e/test_search_v3_stdio.sh"
+fi
+
+if command -v ubs >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  run_stage "scan.ubs.changed_script" "ubs scripts/e2e/post_cutover_cleanup_agent_mail.sh"
+fi
+
+set_status "ok" "post_cutover_cleanup.lane.passed" "Post-cutover cleanup validation lane passed"
+exit 0
